## Analysis (curated)

This run validated `MODE=combined`: three co-located dynomited
instances on floki, each fronting its own backend, all under the
process/network/clock/disk fault schedule, with `dyniak-bench`
driving the dyniak PBC instance concurrently.

- **valkey** (real valkey-server backend): 187,243 ok / 15,491 fail.
  Of those failures, 15,478 are `ft/Unknown` + `ftsug/Unknown` --
  the FT.* RediSearch `index not found` condition after a chaos
  kill wipes the in-memory VectorRegistry. This run used the
  pre-fix workload-driver; commit 05f6b4b adds `index not found`
  to the tolerated FT.* strings so future runs classify this
  (chaos-induced, expected) condition as a tolerated no-op. The
  13 non-FT valkey failures are connection Closed/Timeout during
  process-kill faults.
- **memcache** (real memcached backend): 198,533 ok / 17 fail
  (99.99%); every failure a chaos Closed/Timeout.
- **dyniak** (in-process transactional noxu, PBC + HTTP + QUIC):
  57,273 ok / 17 fail (99.97%); every failure a chaos
  Closed/Timeout. dyniak-bench drove this same instance
  concurrently: 54,038 PBC ops, 0 errors, 180 ops/s, p99 ~243ms.

Net real (chaos-induced) failure rate excluding the FT.*
classification artifact: ~0.03% across all three protocols.

Post-test sweep: coordinator exited 0 with `failed: <none>`; no
orphaned dynomited / driver / injector / backend processes.

The full 4-host combined pass requires valkey + memcached on
arnold (sudo dnf), nuc (sudo pkg), and meh (nix profile conflict
to resolve); floki validated the complete refactored stack
(data_store {valkey, memcache, dyniak}, all dyniak surfaces,
noxu 4.0.0) end to end.
